edges.description: Feed computed planting windows into yield engine so it can infer expected harvest timing and volume.
Use harvest windows (including feast-day highlights) as the primary time buckets for yield and preservation planning.
Align yield predictions with storehouse targets so coverage % can be computed for each crop.
Use household size and months-of-coverage targets to compute per-person, per-month coverage from expected yields.
Convert grouped preservation load (by crop and method) into preservation batch session suggestions.
Summarized weekly harvest load becomes the basis for garden harvest sessions and labor planning.
Storehouse planner updates coverage KPIs using yield-driven coverage %, highlighting gaps and surplus.
Next-best-actions from yield (e.g., thin, side-dress, schedule preservation) become triggers for SSA automation and SessionRunner.
